solution.name: Pong Academy
solution.one_liner: An agentic, self-improving robotics calibration loop: a frozen evaluator scores real-world episodes while an AI coding agent (Karpathy's Autoresearch contract) rewrites the action policy, tests the physical shot, keeps if score improves, rolls back if not.
architecture: Local rig: webcam + ball-hitting servo + on-laptop detector extracting spatial coordinates in real time.
Controller: receives live coordinates over Wi-Fi, commands the servo to intercept the ball.
Episode Manager + MCP server: segments camera stream into discrete measurable episodes (inputs: coords/angles; output: scored or not), exposed to the cloud via MCP.

Policy Optimizer (the Autoresearch loop): cloud agent queries episode history, objective = maximize score; evaluation frozen, agent free to rewrite the action policy; propose -> test physical shot -> check score -> commit if better else rollback.
benefits: Last-mile efficiency: masters the specific physics of a rig in hundreds-to-thousands of high-signal attempts instead of billions of random ones.
Hyper-adaptability: auto fine-tunes to real-world imperfections (wonky camera angles, bent rigs, changing environments) with no human tuning.
Autonomous overnight operation: write the goal in a markdown file, point the agent at the problem, let it run loops.
solution.moat_logic: Treats physical robotic actions as an optimization problem an AI coding agent solves autonomously; the proprietary asset is the high-signal episode dataset + the calibration policies the loop discovers per environment (a data + workflow flywheel), not 'we use AI'.
solution.sota_comparison: vs Google Arm Farm (millions/billions of blind physical runs, dark factories of arms) and Hello Robot Stretch (low-cost roving general data): Pong Academy is ~10k intelligent optimized runs, extremely high-signal, very low resource cost (single rig + API compute).

aleh.role: Founding GTM / Product / commercial operator, AI-native
facts: 7-time founder, 2 exits, $10M+ raised, 10+ patents (coverage through 2041), Techstars & SOSV alum.
$0 -> $5M revenue bootstrapped at 100%FOOD (1,000,000+ servings).
$0 -> $6M bootstrapped at MOBYS Group, managed 150 people.
AB InBev: closed strategic investment + global supply agreement at Planetarians.
Validated unit economics $0.42/lb COGS, $3.35M CapEx at commercial scale (Hayward, CA).

Ships paying-customer products in days with Claude Code / Codex: AI Customs = paying design partner (CDEK franchisee) + 2 live products in 6 weeks, $1.8K MRR, customer requested full legacy replacement.
Nash Compass PMF: 78.7% install conversion, 73% data uploads, 55.6% post-result registrations across 202 users (all ~3x industry benchmark).
Agent Risk Score: 86% CEO buy-in at Techstars validation, 1 LOI signed, Munich Re dialogue initiated.
Quantitative PMF discipline: designs tests with benchmarked go/no-go gates and decision rules (Mastermind Handshake).
Closes first 5-10 enterprise/design-partner customers from cold (AB InBev, CDEK, Aeroflot, Google).
2-4x sales velocity vs category at Planetarians; 72% acceptance in schools & foodservice.
Deep-tech commercialization: translates complex R&D (AI, climate tech, bioprocesses) into fundable unit economics and defensible IP.
Currently Chief of Staff at Dynamic Resonance AI; deep on MCP / agentic-internet / protocol-driven thesis; builds vertical MCP wedges (e.g. MCP-as-a-Service for local service businesses).
MS Robotic Systems Development, Carnegie Mellon University (2017-2018).
BE Aeronautical Engineering, University of Sydney.
Senior Perception Engineer at Andromeda (2026-present).
Perception Engineer at Saronic Technologies (2025-2026, autonomous maritime/defense).
Robotics Research Engineer at L5 Automation (2021-2024).
Robotics Research Engineer at KEF Robotics (2020-2021).
Robotics Engineer at NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (2019-2020).
Robotics intern at Aurora Flight Sciences; engineering intern at SpinLaunch; design engineer/stress analyst at Boeing Aerostructures Australia.
Core expertise: real-world perception, sensor fusion, robotics research engineering across defense, space, and automation.

founders.team_edge: A rare GTM+perception pair: Aleh closes design partners and runs quantified PMF gates in days; Ed builds the perception/robotics core that makes the calibration loop real. Direct lived experience on both the commercial and the sim-to-real sides.

rfps.note: Reputable third-party demand pull standing in for customer tests. Cite the specific RFP that matches each wedge.

items.title: AI for Low-Pesticide Agriculture (Garry Tan)
items.signal: AI now IDs individual weeds/pests in real time; robotics can treat one plant; target cut pesticide 90%; Synphony cites strawberries = $3B in California alone, 60% labor cost = 'generational company'.

items.title: Counter-Swarm Defense (Tyler Bosmeny)
items.signal: Patriot $3M vs FPV drone $500; wants interceptors, sensor-fusion, attacks on the autonomy stack; winners look like Cloudflare not Raytheon.

items.title: Hardware Supply Chain (Nicolas Dessaigne)
items.signal: Shenzhen design->part in a day, US takes weeks; wants dramatically faster parts, tight design/manufacturing/logistics integration.

items.title: Inference Chips for Agent Workflows (Diana Hu)
items.signal: GPUs hit 30-40% utilization on bursty agent workloads; NVIDIA bought Groq for $20B.

items.title: Supply Chain 2.0 for Semiconductors (Diana Hu)
items.signal: 1,400 process steps, dozen countries, 5 months; in 2021 a $300 chip held up $210B of vehicles.

items.title: AI-native industrial base (David Ulevitch)
items.signal: Robotics-heavy manufacturing, next-gen mining, AI coordinating fleets of autonomous machines.

items.title: Renaissance of the American factory (Erin Price-Wright)
items.signal: Factory mindset on energy/mining/construction/manufacturing; modular AI+autonomy alongside skilled workers; deploy autonomy for dangerous tasks; speed design cycles with design-for-manufacture.

items.title: Physical observability (Zabie Elmgren)
items.signal: 1B+ networked cameras/sensors; wants privacy-preserving interoperable AI-native systems that render the physical world as observable as code; enabling layer for robotics/autonomy.

items.title: Electro-industrial stack (Ryan McEntush)
items.signal: Minerals->components, energy->batteries, motion->precision motors, coordinated by software (EVs, drones, datacenters, manufacturing).

items.title: Autonomous labs (Oliver Hsu)
items.signal: As robotic manipulation improves, lights-out labs close the loop from hypothesis to experiment execution. DIRECTLY relevant to an autoresearch product.

items.title: Data crusade in critical industries (Will Bitsky)
items.signal: 2026 defined by data constraints; each truck roll/meter read/production run is training fodder; opening = the coordination stack: collection/annotation/consent, sensor hardware+SDKs, RL environments and training pipelines, eventually intelligent machines.

seed_ideas_reference: Industrial last-mile calibration: arms self-calibrate to a factory station (lighting/vibration/2mm fixture shifts) in ~30 min, write their own micro-adjustments to 99.99% success.
Adaptive sports training tech: smart ball machines (tennis/table tennis/batting) that learn the environment and player, self-correct aim. Direct translation of the existing rig.
Precision agriculture: fruit-picking / weed-zapping robots that adapt actuation timing + camera angles per orchard over first ~100 attempts.
Smart-home appliance personalization: teach-by-doing household robots that learn one user's quirky kitchen layout ('every mom makes the salad differently').
